treatment: Dietary management. Self-monitoring blood glucose x6/day. Targets: fasting <0.95 g/L, 2h postprandial <1.20 g/L
clinical_notes: G2P1 32 yrs. GDM diagnosed on positive OGTT at 24 wks. No diabetes history. Pre-pregnancy BMI 25.2. Growth ultrasound normal. Started SMBG and dietary plan. Follow-up 15 days.
Good glycemic control on diet alone. Mean fasting 0.88, mean postprandial 1.10. Continue monitoring.

treatment: Diet + SMBG. Methyldopa 250mg twice daily initiated.
clinical_notes: New onset hypertension. BP 145/92. Proteinuria at 35 mg/mmol (lab result 09/12). Started methyldopa 250mg morning and evening. Liver function and CBC normal. Close monitoring. If worsening -> hospitalization. Warning signs explained.

treatment: Methyldopa 250mg three times daily (dose increase). Betamethasone 12mg IM x2 (fetal lung maturation)
clinical_notes: BP poorly controlled on methyldopa 500mg/day. Increased to 750mg/day. Rising proteinuria. Ultrasound: moderate oligohydramnios, borderline Dopplers. Corticosteroids for fetal lung maturation (2 doses 24h apart). Hospitalize if BP >160/100 or symptoms develop. Patient understands warning signs well.

clinical_notes: G1P0, spontaneous pregnancy. No significant history. Normal exam. Continue midwife + specialist follow-up.
